official_name_short: Z-Mauritians Unite — Civic Truth & Dignity Engine

introduction.peaceful_statement: Z-Mauritians Unite is a peaceful civic evidence posture inside the Sanctuary hub: verify claims before voice, privilege dignity, route people toward lawful pathways, and avoid group blame or unverified accusations against named persons.
introduction.raw_material_policy: Emotional documents may inform themes only. They MUST NOT ship as authoritative until every row passes source and peace checks.

claims.claim_summary: Workers seeking redress deserve clear pointers to lawful national complaint and representation routes.
claims.public_wording: Many workplace issues belong in formal labour grievance pathways. Operators should cite current Ministry guidance and statutes when advising next steps—not social rumor.
claims.next_verification_step: Refresh links to official labour portals quarterly; annotate dates on any cited circulars.

claims.claim_summary: Wage indices and headline minimum wage adjustments must be anchored to dated official or gazetted sources—not reposts alone.
claims.public_wording: We are compiling official wage series and minimum wage regulations with retrieval dates. No headline number is affirmed here until the ledger row cites the legal instrument or official statistics release.
claims.next_verification_step: Pull Statistics Mauritius labour publications and Gazette wage orders; store citations in a future appendix row—not in this Phase 1 slice.

claims.claim_summary: Household debt stress is socially salient but needs transparent methodology before any civic dashboard posts comparisons.
claims.public_wording: Debt burden narratives require defined populations, reputable sources, and sensitivity; this hub stores research intent only—not advice or shaming.
claims.next_verification_step: Commission literature review Rank A–C sources; forbid individual shaming narratives.

claims.claim_summary: International human rights mechanisms are procedural; expectations must stay precise and lawful.
claims.public_wording: Treaty mechanisms may offer communications procedures where applicable—they are slow, procedural, and not substitutes for courts. Operators must read OHCHR guidance verbatim before summarizing.
claims.next_verification_step: Pair each pathway summary with verbatim OHCHR or treaty excerpts plus AMK/ethics gate for distribution.
